fact: Google Play, also known as the Google Play Store or Play Store, and formerly known as the Android Market, is a digital distribution service operated and developed by Google.

subtitle: Website
fact: Library Genesis is a shadow library project for file-sharing access to scholarly journal articles, academic and general-interest books, images, comics, audiobooks, and magazines.

subtitle: Website
fact: VK is a Russian online social media and social networking service based in Saint Petersburg.

title: ‘Outlook.com’ launches
subtitle: Website
fact: Outlook.com, formerly Hotmail, is a free personal email service offered by Microsoft.

title: ‘CNN’ launches
subtitle: Website
fact: The Cable News Network (CNN) is an American multinational news media company and the flagship namesake property of CNN Worldwide, a division of Warner Bros.

title: ‘Discord’ launches
subtitle: Website
fact: Discord is an instant messaging and VoIP social platform that allows communication through voice calls, video calls, text messaging, and media.

title: ‘Gemini’ launches
subtitle: Website
fact: Gemini is a generative artificial intelligence chatbot and virtual assistant developed by Google.

title: ‘Google Translate’ launches
subtitle: Website
fact: Google Translate is a multilingual neural machine translation service developed by Google to translate text, documents and websites from one language into another.

title: ‘Sci-Hub’ launches
subtitle: Website
fact: Sci-Hub is a shadow library that provides free access to millions of research papers, regardless of copyright, by bypassing publishers' paywalls in various ways.

title: ‘PayPal’ launches
subtitle: Website
fact: PayPal Holdings, Inc. is an American multinational financial technology company operating an online payments system in the majority of countries that support online money transfers; it serves as an electronic alternative to traditional paper methods such as checks and money orders.

title: ‘EBay’ launches
subtitle: Website
fact: eBay Inc. is an American multinational e-commerce company based in San Jose, California, that allows users to buy or view items via retail sales through online marketplaces and websites in 190 markets worldwide.

title: ‘Rotten Tomatoes’ launches
subtitle: Website
fact: Rotten Tomatoes is an American review-aggregation website for film and television.

title: ‘Google Docs’ launches
subtitle: Website
fact: Google Docs is an online word processor and part of the free, web-based Google Docs Editors suite offered by Google.

title: ‘Google Scholar’ launches
subtitle: Website

title: ‘MSN’ launches
subtitle: Website
fact: MSN is a web portal and related collection of Internet services and apps provided by Microsoft.

title: ‘Hulu’ launches
subtitle: Website

title: ‘IMDb’ launches
subtitle: Website

subtitle: Website

fact: Flipkart Inc. is an Indian e-commerce company, headquartered in Bengaluru, and incorporated in Singapore as a private limited company.

title: ‘Bluesky’ launches
subtitle: Website
fact: Bluesky is an American microblogging social media service.

title: ‘Alibaba Group’ launches
subtitle: Website
fact: Alibaba Group Holding Limited, branded as Alibaba, is a Chinese multinational technology company specializing in e-commerce, retail, Internet, and technology.

title: ‘Airbnb’ launches
subtitle: Website
fact: Airbnb, Inc. is an American company operating an online marketplace for short-and-long-term homestays, experiences and services in various countries and regions.

title: ‘Microsoft Bing’ launches
subtitle: Website
fact: Microsoft Bing is a search engine owned and operated by Microsoft, and developed by Microsoft AI.

title: ‘Voice of America’ launches
subtitle: Website
fact: Voice of America is an international broadcaster, funded by the United States federal government and established in 1942.

title: ‘Bilibili’ launches
subtitle: Website
